headline: 9 core program and account addresses catalogued; dzSOL liquid staking token and SPL stake pool with distributed authorities
short_headline: 9 addresses: dzSOL token, stake pool, authorities

findings.text: Solana Compass identified stake pool at 3fV1sdGeXaNEZj6EPDTpub82pYxcRXwt2oie6jkSzeWi with dzSOL token at Gekfj7SL2fVpTDxJZmeC46cTYxinjB6gkAnb6EGT6mnn; uses spl-stake-pool on-chain program

Solana Compass stake pool detail page lists 9 associated addresses: program, manager, staker, deposit authority, withdraw authority, validator list storage, pool mint, manager fee account, and reserve stake
No audit links provided by protocol; search of news and documentation yielded no security audit reports

No GitHub repository found in pinned metadata; protocol website unreachable; documentation available via news and education sites (Chainflow, Cherry Servers, DataWallet) but no deployment artifacts discovered
Protocol operates on Solana only (single chain); discovery complete for primary chain
Stake pool is a consumer of the spl-stake-pool factory, not a factory itself; does not enumerate child contracts
Role taxonomy: dzSOL token (token), stake pool program (pool), manager/staker/authorities (admin), reserve stake (vault), 2Z governance token (token)
All 9 addresses traced to Solana Compass fetched page; 2Z token address traced to Gate.io and memecoinstools.com announcements

rationale.verdict: Discovery completed. Identified 10 primary addresses: 8 from spl-stake-pool (dzSOL token contract, stake pool program, manager, staker, deposit authority, withdraw authority, validator list storage, manager fee account, reserve stake), plus 2Z governance token. Website and GitHub unavailable; no prior audit reports located. Role classifications assigned per taxonomy.

evidence.shows: Stake pool program address 3fV1sdGeXaNEZj6EPDTpub82pYxcRXwt2oie6jkSzeWi, dzSOL token Gekfj7SL2fVpTDxJZmeC46cTYxinjB6gkAnb6EGT6mnn, and 9 associated account addresses (manager, staker, deposit authority, withdraw authority, validator list, pool mint, manager fee, reserve stake); uses spl-stake-pool program
dzSOL token contract address Gekfj7SL2fVpTDxJZmeC46cTYxinjB6gkAnb6EGT6mnn on Solana; verified token per Solana token registry
2Z native token contract address J6pQQ3FAcJQeWPPGppWRb4nM8jU3wLyYbRrLh7feMfvd announced for October 2, 2025 issuance on Solana
2Z token launched October 2, 2025 as native utility token of DoubleZero protocol on Solana; 10 billion total supply with inflation and burning mechanics; not a governance token at launch

DoubleZero protocol operates with on-chain smart contracts for activation and coordination; DZ CLI calls smart contract program to register validators; system includes activator service, controller orchestration, and agent components listening to blockchain events
DoubleZero launched 3M SOL stake pool under ticker dzSOL; protocol charges 5% of validators' consensus-related revenue for network access; founder Austin Federa from Solana Labs

DoubleZero architecture includes two-ring structure: Outer Ring for edge filtration with FPGA-based signature verification and packet deduplication; Inner Ring optimized core with low-latency dedicated bandwidth; DoubleZero Devices (DZDs) terminate fiber links and run Agent software per smart contract instructions

Protocol description: 'decentralized framework for creating and managing high-performance permissionless networks, optimized for distributed systems like blockchain'; users stake SOL to earn dzSOL liquid staking tokens

unknowns: D2: deployed_contracts_doc – no canonical deployments page located; website unreachable
D3: audit_links – no security audits discovered; none recorded in protocol metadata
D4: GitHub – no repository URL found in search results or pinned metadata; code visibility unavailable
D4: deployment_artifacts – no broadcast/, deployments/, or addresses.json files discovered; protocol source code not accessible

protocol_metadata.about: DoubleZero Staked SOL (dzSOL) is a liquid staking pool on Solana that allows users to stake SOL and earn dzSOL receipt tokens while participating in the DoubleZero network—a high-performance fiber backbone infrastructure designed to reduce validator latency. The protocol delegates staked SOL across geographically distributed validators on the DoubleZero testnet and mainnet-beta, charging 5% of consensus rewards in exchange for access to low-latency networking. The native 2Z utility token launched October 2, 2025, and is used for network access payments and contributor incentives rather than governance.
